codeReview.summary: The fix commit only records validation artifacts and does not change switcher test/source or target-membership wiring, so it does not remediate the prior blocking failures from integration-hardening-add-ui-level-cross-surface-contract-tests.

issues.description: Prior blocking gaps (UI-level cross-surface behavioral assertions, actionable CTA routing assertions, and runtime-log-path redaction validation) remain unaddressed by this fix. The reviewed fix commit does not modify this file, so the previously failed coverage remains unresolved.

The fix commit includes unrelated factory/library and validation-artifact updates instead of persistent discovery hardening in app/test project files (e.g., test target membership/build config). This weakens traceability between the feature objective and committed implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked followedProcedure=true, but the handoff did not execute the full validation stack listed in switcher-integration-worker (swift test + app script + relevant smoke), only scoped xcodebuild commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md step 5 requires the full validation stack; handoff for session 824808b6-50fc-41d7-86c5-f20c7ffc406a lists only scoped xcodebuild runs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The derived-data cache/discovery pitfall (tests present but not discovered until using a fresh derivedDataPath) appears operationally important and recurring, but it is not captured in mission AGENTS known constraints.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature precondition and handoff explicitly reference Xcode cached test bundle causing discovery miss; mission AGENTS known constraints mention destination/signing issues but not discovery-cache behavior.

summary: Review failed. The fix commit demonstrates scoped test execution evidence under a fresh derived data path, but it does not implement code/project changes that resolve the original failed feature’s blocking assertions, so the prior failure is not adequately addressed by this fix alone.
